name: Herb Alpert
description: American trumpeter and recording industry executive (born 1935)
abstract: Herb Alpert (born March 31, 1935) is an American musician who led the band Herb Alpert & the Tijuana Brass (sometimes called Herb Alpert and the TJB or Herb Alpert’s Tijuana Brass) in the 1960s. During the same decade, he co-founded A&M Records with Jerry Moss.

concerts.label: Herb Alpert and the Tijuana Brass
Herb Alpert and the Tijuana Brass

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Jolly
about_quotes.evidence: I was on “This Guy’s In Love With You” with Herb and that was on— ... And “A Taste of Honey,” I was on that one. ... Oh yeah with Herb Alpert?
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Jolly interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was with Herb Alpert’s re-formed... It was fun. That was a hot band... We traveled around for a couple of years
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Wes Montgomery who got us on A&M Records. How he did it was, he asked Herb Alpert one day... Wes was a star... before he died, he said to Herb Alpert – he said, 'You ever heard of a kid named George Benson?'... When he died, Herb Alpert said to Creed Taylor... 'Do you know a kid named George Benson?
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee responds to the interviewer's mention of Herb Alpert by agreeing with the classification of his music ('Right. And that’s okay.'), but provides no personal interaction, meeting, or professional connection.
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Thomas interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: wherever he goes he can use it, you know. And that was a very -- I have to give Quincy a lot of credit on this. That Sunday, we were at A&M recording studios, you know, -- where Herb Alpert is.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Strangely enough, I was in New Orleans one time, and you know this boy who's got the Tijuana Brass, Herb Alpert or whatever his name is...? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah.
